mission.title: tikv: Async commit does not ensure linearizability
mission.description: Async commit does not ensure linearizability. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify tikv troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your tikv deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv -n tikv --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review tikv configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant tikv configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl get configmap -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=tikv
```
### Read in the same session

Read with max u64 immediately after an async commit transaction. Because the lock in the previous transaction may be not committed yet, and locks can be ignored if we read with max u64.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Async commit does not ensure linearizability
steps.description:
> > 
> > A more concrete example:
> > ```
> > 1. An external client starts two sessions A and B.
> > 
> > 2. Session A starts T1, start_ts = 1.
> > 
> > 3. Session B starts T2, start_ts = 2.
> > 
> > 4. Client starts session C, it starts a transaction T3, start_ts = 80.
> > 
> > 5. Session A prewrites k1=1.
> > 
> > 6. Session A commits T1 with commit_ts = 100.
> > 
> > 7. Session B prewrites k2=1 using async commit, min_commit_ts = 51.
> > 
> > 8. Session B commits T2 with commit_ts = 51.
> >

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Async commit does not ensure linearizability is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv -n tikv --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n tikv --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
> > 
> > A more concrete example:
> > ```
> > 1. An external client starts two sessions A and B.
> > 
> > 2. Session A starts T1, start_ts = 1.
> > 
> > 3. Session B starts T2, start_ts = 2.
> > 
> > 4. Client starts session C, it starts a transaction T3, start_ts = 80.
> > 
> > 5. Session A prewrites k1=1.
> > 
> > 6. Session A commits T1 with commit_ts = 100.
> > 
> > 7.

See the source issue and linked PR for the complete implementation details.

prerequisites.description: A running TiKV cluster (standalone or deployed on Kubernetes via TiDB Operator). TiUP and tikv-ctl are the primary CLI tools for managing TiKV.
